heading: Plan and authority to assist local educational agencies experiencing growth in enrollment due to force structure changes, relocation of military units, or base closures and realignments
label: 7703c Plan and authority to assist local educational agencies experiencing growth in enrollment due to force structure changes, relocation of military units, or base closures and realignments

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “During the period beginning on , and ending on , the Secretary of Defense shall work collaboratively with the Secretary of Education in any efforts to ease the transition of military dependent students from attendance in Department of Defense dependent schools to attendance in schools of local educational agencies. The Secretary of Defense may use funds of the Department of Defense Education Activity to share expertise and experience of the Activity with local educational agencies as military dependent students make such transition, including such a transition resulting from the closure or realignment of military installations under a base closure law, global rebasing, and force restructuring.”Pub. A, title V, § 552(b)131 Stat. 1401Dec. 23, 2016Dec. 12, 2017“The budget justification materials that accompany any budget of the President for a fiscal year after fiscal year 2017 (as submitted to Congress pursuant to , United States Code) shall include, with respect to section 574(c) of the John Warner National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2007 (; note [now ]), the following:section 1105 of title 31Public Law 109–36420 U.S.C. 7703b20 U.S.C. 7703c“(1)A full accounting of the expenditure of funds pursuant to such section 574(c) during the last fiscal year ending before the date of the submittal of the budget.“(2)An assessment of the impact of the expenditure of such funds on the quality of opportunities for elementary and secondary education made available for military dependent students.”

children.text: Force structure changes.
The relocation of a military unit.
The closure or realignment of military installations pursuant to defense base closure and realignment under the base closure laws.

children.label: A
children.text: an identification of the military installations affected by such arrivals and departures;

children.label: B
children.text: an estimate of the number of such students arriving at or departing from each such installation; and

children.label: C
children.text: the anticipated schedule of such arrivals and departures.

Such recommendations as the Office of Economic Adjustment of the Department of Defense considers appropriate for means of assisting affected local educational agencies in accommodating increases in enrollment of military dependent students as a result of any such event.

A plan for outreach to be conducted to affected local educational agencies, commanders of military installations, and members of the Armed Forces and civilian personnel of the Department of Defense regarding information on the assistance to be provided under the plan under subsection (a).

children.heading: Transition of military dependents among local educational agencies
children.label: c Transition of military dependents among local educational agencies

children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall work collaboratively with the Secretary of Education in any efforts to ease the transitions of military dependent students from Department of Defense dependent schools to other schools and among schools of local educational agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: To share expertise and experience of the Activity with local educational agencies as military dependent students make the transitions described in paragraph (1), including transitions resulting from the closure or realignment of military installations under a base closure law, global rebasing, and force restructuring.

children.label: i
children.text: Access to virtual and distance learning capabilities and related applications.

children.label: ii
children.text: Training for teachers.

children.label: iii
children.text: Academic strategies to increase academic achievement.

children.label: iv
children.text: Curriculum development.

children.label: v
children.text: Support for practices that minimize the impact of transition and deployment.

children.label: vi
children.text: Other appropriate services to improve the academic achievement of such students.
The term “base closure law” has the meaning given that term in .section 101 of title 10
The term “local educational agency” has the meaning given that term in .section 7713(9) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: elementary and secondary school students who are dependents of members of the Armed Forces;

children.label: B
children.text: elementary and secondary school students who are dependents of civilian employees of the Department of Defense; and

children.label: C
children.text: elementary and secondary school students who are dependents of personnel who are not members of the Armed Forces or civilian employees of the Department of Defense but who are employed on Federal property.
